function.name: Bash
function.description: Executes a given bash command and returns its output. The working directory persists between commands, but shell state does not. IMPORTANT: Avoid using this tool to run find, grep, cat, head, tail, sed, awk, or echo commands. Instead, use the appropriate dedicated tool. While the Bash tool can do similar things, it's better to use the built-in tools as they provide a better user experience.

description.description: Clear, concise description of what this command does in active voice

function.name: Read
function.description: Reads a file from the local filesystem. You can access any file directly by using this tool. The file_path parameter must be an absolute path, not a relative path. By default, it reads up to 2000 lines starting from the beginning of the file. When you already know which part of the file you need, only read that part. Results are returned using cat -n format, with line numbers starting at 1.

file_path.description: The absolute path to the file to read

offset.description: The line number to start reading from. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once

limit.description: The number of lines to read. Only provide if the file is too large to read at once.

function.name: Write
function.description: Writes a file to the local filesystem. This tool will overwrite the existing file if there is one at the provided path. If this is an existing file, you MUST use the Read tool first to read the file's contents. Prefer the Edit tool for modifying existing files. Only use this tool to create new files or for complete rewrites.

file_path.description: The absolute path to the file to write (must be absolute, not relative)

content.description: The content to write to the file

function.name: Edit
function.description: Performs exact string replacements in files. You must use your Read tool at least once in the conversation before editing. The edit will FAIL if old_string is not unique in the file. Either provide a larger string with more surrounding context to make it unique or use replace_all to change every instance of old_string.

file_path.description: The absolute path to the file to modify

new_string.description: The text to replace it with (must be different from old_string)

replace_all.description: Replace all occurrences of old_string (default false)

function.name: Glob
function.description: Fast file pattern matching tool that works with any codebase size. Supports glob patterns like '**/*.js' or 'src/**/*.ts'. Returns matching file paths sorted by modification time. Use this tool when you need to find files by name patterns.

pattern.description: The glob pattern to match files against

path.description: The directory to search in. If not specified, the current working directory will be used.

function.name: Grep
function.description: A powerful search tool built on ripgrep. Supports full regex syntax (e.g., 'log.*Error', 'function\s+\w+'). Filter files with glob parameter (e.g., '*.js', '**/*.tsx') or type parameter (e.g., 'js', 'py', 'rust'). Output modes: 'content' shows matching lines, 'files_with_matches' shows only file paths (default), 'count' shows match counts.

pattern.description: The regular expression pattern to search for in file contents

path.description: File or directory to search in. Defaults to current working directory.

context.description: Number of lines to show before and after each match

function.name: WebFetch
function.description: Fetches content from a URL. Can retrieve HTML pages, API responses, and other web content. Returns the page content as text.

url.description: The URL to fetch content from

prompt.description: Optional prompt to guide content extraction

function.name: WebSearch
function.description: Searches the web for information. Returns search results with titles, URLs, and snippets.

domain.description: Optional domain to restrict search to

function.name: Task
function.description: Launch a new agent to handle complex, multi-step tasks autonomously. The Task tool launches specialized agents that autonomously handle complex tasks. Each agent operates independently and returns results when complete. Provide clear, detailed prompts so the agent can work autonomously and return exactly the information you need.

description.description: A short (3-5 word) description of the task

prompt.description: The task for the agent to perform

function.name: TodoWrite
function.description: Write and manage a list of todo items for tracking tasks. Each item has an id, content, and status (pending, in_progress, or completed). Use this tool frequently to plan and track progress on tasks.

id.description: Unique identifier for the todo item

status.description: Current status of the todo item

todos.description: List of todo items to write

function.name: TodoRead
function.description: Read the current list of todo items to check progress and status of tasks.

function.name: NotebookEdit
function.description: Edit a Jupyter notebook cell. Replaces the source of a specific cell with new content.

notebook_path.description: The absolute path to the Jupyter notebook file

new_source.description: The new source content for the cell
